what: Bayesian Estimation of Multilevel Vector Autoregressive Networks using STAN
title: Bayesian Estimation of Dynamic VAR Models using STAN
text: ‘bvarnet’ package allowes user estimate bayesian multilevel Vector Auto Regressive (VAR) models binary, ordinal continuous outcome variables. Missing data handled listwise deletion skip-lag mechanism, skips estimation temporal structure gap two timepoints. , provide functionality perform hypothesis test perform predictions.

title: Bayesian Estimation of Dynamic VAR Models using STAN
text: can install development version bvarnet GitHub :

title: Bind arrays along the third dimension (simple abind replacement) — abind_simple

what: Compute Bayes factor table for a bvarnet model — bf_table
title: Compute Bayes factor table for a bvarnet model — bf_table
text: Computes Savage-Dickey density ratio Bayes factors parameter requested subset returns tidy data frame.

title: Compute Bayes factor table for a bvarnet model — bf_table
Compute Bayes factor table for a bvarnet model — bf_table
text: object bvarnet object returned bvar(). type Character vector "" (default).  parameter groups test. Options: "ar" (autoregressive), "cl" (cross-lagged), "intercepts", "fe" (non-intercept fixed effects), "lag_fe" (lag × predictor interaction joint tests), "temporal" (joint test phi parameters across lags, .e. entire temporal structure AR + CL, excluding covariates; additionally emits separate joint rows AR-CL-components; lag × covariate interactions present, additional rows emitted per-interaction-term AR-/ CL-interaction sub-tests, plus full temporal + interactions omnibus). "" auto-selects applicable types (skips "intercepts" ordinal models "lag_fe" lag interactions exist). lag Integer; lag block use (default 1). Applies "ar", "cl", "phi". null_value Numeric scalar; null hypothesis value (default 0).

title: Compute Bayes factor table for a bvarnet model — bf_table
text: data frame columns: type, predictor, outcome, BF01, BF10, log_BF01, post_density, prior_density, method.

title: Compute Bayes factor table for a bvarnet model — bf_table
text: type = "fe" "intercepts", table contains three levels: per-cell (logspline), per-predictor joint (MVN), global joint-(MVN).  type = "ar" "cl", existing two-level structure (per-cell + per-type joint) unchanged. type = "lag_fe" emits grouped joint rows lag × predictor interaction terms: per-lag-block full-term omnibus.  Per-cell rows parameters already included type = "fe" requested.

what: Fit a Bayesian multilevel VAR network model — bvar
title: Fit a Bayesian multilevel VAR network model — bvar
text: Compiles samples appropriate Stan model chosen family, extracts results plain base-R objects, returns bvarnet object.

title: Fit a Bayesian multilevel VAR network model — bvar
code: bvar(   id_col,   time_col,   y_cols,   x_cols,   center_x = FALSE,   fe_interactions = NULL,   re_interactions = NULL,   re_cols = NULL,   re_temporal = FALSE,   K = 1,   na_action = c("listwise"),   skip_lag = TRUE,   data,   family = c("bernoulli", "ordinal", "gaussian"),   priors = set_priors(),   iter = 4000,   warmup = 1000,   chains = 4,   cores = 1,   seed = NULL,   adapt_delta = NULL,   max_treedepth = NULL )

title: Fit a Bayesian multilevel VAR network model — bvar
text: id_col Character. Name subject/group identifier column. time_col Character. Name time column. y_cols Character vector. Names outcome columns. x_cols Character vector. Names covariate columns. center_x Logical. Grand-mean centre covariates fitting? Default FALSE. fe_interactions List NULL. Fixed-effect interaction terms add design matrix. element character vector column names interact, c("lag", "x") interact lag columns covariate. re_interactions List NULL. Random-effect interaction terms. re_cols Character vector. Columns X include random slopes. re_temporal Logical. Include random slopes lag predictors? Default FALSE. K Integer. AR order. Default 1. na_action Character. Missing-data strategy; currently "listwise". skip_lag Logical. TRUE (default), rows irregular time gaps lag set zero rather dropped. data Data frame long format. family Character scalar vector. Observation model per node. scalar recycled y_cols. vector length length(y_cols) (named positional) specifies per-node families. Valid values: "bernoulli", "ordinal", "gaussian". priors bvarnet_priors object set_priors(). Defaults set_priors() (package defaults). iter Integer. Number post-warmup iterations per chain. Default 4000. warmup Integer. Number warmup iterations per chain. Default 1000. chains Integer. Number MCMC chains. Default 4. cores Integer. Number chains run parallel. Default 1. seed Integer NULL. RNG seed. adapt_delta Numeric (0, 1). Target average proposal acceptance probability warmup adaptation. Higher values (e.g., 0.95–0.99) reduce divergences cost slower sampling. Default NULL (CmdStan default 0.8). max_treedepth Integer. Maximum depth NUTS binary tree. Increasing allows sampler take leapfrog steps per iteration, can help difficult posteriors (e.g., funnels hierarchical logistic models) increases computation. Default NULL (CmdStan default 10).

title: Fit a Bayesian multilevel VAR network model — bvar
text: bvarnet object (named list) slots: draws, convergence, diagnostics, timing, metadata, return_codes, family, standata, priors.

what: Compare fitted model parameters to simulation truth — compare_to_truth
title: Compare fitted model parameters to simulation truth — compare_to_truth
text: Extracts posterior summaries fitted bvarnet object compares true parameter values used data generation.

title: Compare fitted model parameters to simulation truth — compare_to_truth
code: compare_to_truth(   fit,   truth,   ci_width = 0.9,   bayes_factor = FALSE,   null_value = 0 )

title: Compare fitted model parameters to simulation truth — compare_to_truth
text: fit fitted bvarnet object (output bvar()). truth truth component sim_var() output. ci_width Numeric. Width credible interval (default 0.90). bayes_factor Logical; TRUE, compute Savage-Dickey BFs beta phi parameters append BF01, BF10, bf_correct columns.  bf_correct TRUE BF01 > 1 true null parameters (true value == null_value) BF10 > 1 true non-null parameters.  Default FALSE. null_value Numeric scalar; null hypothesis value Bayes factor computation (default 0).  used bayes_factor = TRUE.

title: Compare fitted model parameters to simulation truth — compare_to_truth
text: data frame columns: parameter, node, true_value, post_mean, post_sd, ci_lower, ci_upper, covered (logical), optionally BF01, BF10, bf_correct.

title: Extract all posterior draws of subject-level random effects u — .extract_u_draws

what: Predict from a fitted bvarnet model — predict.bvarnet
title: Predict from a fitted bvarnet model — predict.bvarnet
text: Computes one-step-ahead recursive forecasts long-format time-series data. Supports population-level (subject_re = "zero") subject-specific (subject_re = "posterior-mean") predictions. Also serves --sample engine: fit training data, call predict(fit, newdata = test_data).

title: Predict from a fitted bvarnet model — predict.bvarnet
code: # S3 method for class 'bvarnet' predict(   object,   newdata = NULL,   forecast = c("one-step", "recursive"),   conditioning_window = NULL,   type = c("link", "response", "probabilities"),   method = c("posterior-mean", "posterior-sample"),   ndraws = NULL,   seed = NULL,   subject_re = c("zero", "posterior-mean", "sample"),   new_subject = c("zero", "sample"),   ... )

title: Predict from a fitted bvarnet model — predict.bvarnet
text: object bvarnet object bvar(). newdata Data frame long format. NULL, original training data design matrices (stored object$standata) used -sample fitted values. forecast Character. "one-step" (default) uses observed lag values newdata every row. "recursive" feeds predicted values back lag buffer conditioning window. conditioning_window Integer scalar, named integer vector, NULL. Number observed time points (per subject) used initialise lag buffer recursive forecasting begins. Must >= K. NULL, defaults K (minimum lag history). Ignored forecast = "one-step". type Character. Output type: "link" (linear predictor), "response" (mean outcome scale), "probabilities" (category-level probabilities/mean+sd). method Character. "posterior-mean" uses posterior means (deterministic). "posterior-sample" averages ndraws draws returns attr(,"sd") across-draw SD. ndraws Integer. Number posterior draws use method = "posterior-sample". Defaults smaller 100 total available draws. seed Integer NULL. RNG seed draw selection new-subject sampling. subject_re Character. handle random effects: "zero" (population-level, u = 0), "posterior-mean" (posterior mean u seen subjects), "sample" (draw-specific u seen subjects). new_subject Character. Fallback unseen subjects: "zero" (u = 0) "sample" (draw RE distribution). ... Ignored.

title: Predict from a fitted bvarnet model — predict.bvarnet
text: type = "link" "response": numeric matrix rows matching original data (nrow(newdata)), NA first K rows per subject. type = "probabilities": list p matrices. method = "posterior-sample", output carries attr(,"sd") attr(,"ndraws").

title: Predict from a fitted bvarnet model — predict.bvarnet
code: if (instantiate::stan_cmdstan_exists()) {   sim <- sim_var(N = 5, T_obs = 30, p = 2, family = "gaussian", seed = 1)   fit <- bvar(id_col = "id", time_col = "t",               y_cols = c("y_1", "y_2"), x_cols = character(0),               data = sim$data, family = "gaussian",               iter = 200, warmup = 100, chains = 2, seed = 1)   preds <- predict(fit, type = "response") }

title: Build a complete prior specification for bvarnet — set_priors

title: Build a complete prior specification for bvarnet — set_priors
code: set_priors(beta = NULL, phi = NULL, sd_u = NULL, kappa = NULL, sigma = NULL)

title: Build a complete prior specification for bvarnet — set_priors
text: beta Prior fixed-effect regression coefficients. phi Prior lag coefficients. sd_u Prior random-effect standard deviations (half-prior). kappa Prior ordinal cut-points (ordinal models ). sigma Prior residual standard deviation (gaussian models ; half-prior).

title: Build a complete prior specification for bvarnet — set_priors
text: bvarnet_priors S3 object.

what: Simulate data from a multilevel VAR model — sim_var
title: Simulate data from a multilevel VAR model — sim_var
text: Generates data generative model implied Stan model family. Useful testing parameter recovery model validation.

title: Simulate data from a multilevel VAR model — sim_var
code: sim_var(   N,   T_obs,   p,   K = 1L,   family = c("bernoulli", "ordinal", "gaussian"),   alpha = NULL,   gamma = NULL,   Phi = NULL,   sigma = NULL,   kappa = NULL,   q = 0L,   x_gen = NULL,   sd_alpha = 0.5,   sd_phi = 0.2,   sd_gamma = NULL,   re_temporal = FALSE,   C = 5L,   burnin = 500L,   seed = NULL )

title: Simulate data from a multilevel VAR model — sim_var
text: N Integer. Number subjects (groups). T_obs Integer. Number time points per subject. p Integer. Number outcome nodes. K Integer. AR order (default 1). family Character. One "bernoulli", "ordinal", "gaussian". alpha Numeric vector length p. Population intercepts (logit scale bernoulli, identity gaussian). ordinal, absorbed kappa left NULL. Generated NULL. gamma Matrix q x p. Population covariate effects. Generated NULL q > 0. Phi Matrix (p*K) x p. Population lag coefficients. Generated NULL. sigma Numeric vector length p. Residual SD per node (gaussian ). Generated NULL. kappa List p ordered vectors, length C-1. Cutpoints per node (ordinal ). Generated NULL. q Integer. Number covariates (default 0). x_gen Function f(N, T_obs) returning N x T_obs x q array covariates. NULL, default generation used. sd_alpha Numeric. SD random intercepts (scalar p-vector). Default 0.5. Set 0 simulate fixed-effects-model -person variation intercepts. sd_phi Numeric. SD random lag coefficients (scalar matrix). Default 0.2. sd_gamma Numeric NULL. SD random covariate slopes. NULL means random slopes covariates. re_temporal Logical. Include random slopes lag predictors? Default FALSE. C Integer. Number ordinal categories (ordinal , default 5). burnin Integer. Number time points discard warmup recording data (default 500). VAR process simulated burnin + T_obs time points per subject, first burnin discarded. allows process reach stationary distribution data collection begins. seed Integer NULL. RNG seed.

title: Simulate data from a multilevel VAR model — sim_var
text: list two components: data long-format data frame columns id, t, y_1, ..., y_p, optionally x_1, ..., x_q. truth list true generating parameters.

title: Simulate data from a multilevel VAR model — sim_var
text: simulate VAR without random effects (.e. subjects share identical parameters), set sd_alpha = 0, re_temporal = FALSE (default), sd_gamma = NULL (default).

what: Simulate new trajectories from a fitted bvarnet model — simulate.bvarnet
title: Simulate new trajectories from a fitted bvarnet model — simulate.bvarnet
text: Generates new multilevel VAR trajectories using posterior parameters. Reuses existing response kernels sim_var().

title: Simulate new trajectories from a fitted bvarnet model — simulate.bvarnet
code: # S3 method for class 'bvarnet' simulate(   object,   nsim = 20L,   seed = NULL,   method = c("posterior-mean", "posterior-sample"),   ndraws = 10L,   N = NULL,   burnin = 200L,   x_gen = NULL,   subject_re = c("sample", "zero"),   ... )

title: Simulate new trajectories from a fitted bvarnet model — simulate.bvarnet
text: object bvarnet object bvar(). nsim Integer. Number time points per subject. Default 20. seed Integer NULL. RNG seed. method Character. "posterior-mean" returns one data frame. "posterior-sample" returns list data frames (one per draw). ndraws Integer. Number posterior draws method = "posterior-sample". Default 10. N Integer. Number subjects. Defaults J fitted model. burnin Integer. Burn-time points discard. Default 200. x_gen Function f(N, T) returning N x T x q array covariates, NULL zero covariates. subject_re Character. "sample" draws REs N(0, sd_u). "zero" sets REs 0. ... Ignored.

title: Simulate new trajectories from a fitted bvarnet model — simulate.bvarnet
text: method = "posterior-mean": data frame columns id, t, y_1, ..., y_p (optional x_1, ..., x_q). "posterior-sample": list data frames, one per draw.

title: Simulate new trajectories from a fitted bvarnet model — simulate.bvarnet
code: if (instantiate::stan_cmdstan_exists()) {   sim <- sim_var(N = 5, T_obs = 30, p = 2, family = "gaussian", seed = 1)   fit <- bvar(id_col = "id", time_col = "t",               y_cols = c("y_1", "y_2"), x_cols = character(0),               data = sim$data, family = "gaussian",               iter = 200, warmup = 100, chains = 2, seed = 1)   sim_data <- simulate(fit, nsim = 20, seed = 42) }

what: Build a Stan data list from a long-format data frame — to_stan_data
title: Build a Stan data list from a long-format data frame — to_stan_data
text: Internal function called bvar(). Constructs list passed Stan model: design matrices Y, X, B, Z; dimensions; prior hyperparameters derived bvarnet_priors object.

title: Build a Stan data list from a long-format data frame — to_stan_data
code: to_stan_data(   data,   family,   id_col,   time_col,   y_cols,   x_cols,   center_x = F,   fe_interactions = NULL,   re_interactions = NULL,   re_cols = character(0),   re_temporal = FALSE,   K,   na_action = c("listwise"),   skip_lag = TRUE,   priors = set_priors() )

title: Build a Stan data list from a long-format data frame — to_stan_data
text: data Data frame long format. family Character. "bernoulli", "ordinal", "gaussian". id_col Character. Subject/group identifier column name. time_col Character. Time column name. y_cols Character vector. Outcome column names. x_cols Character vector. Covariate column names. center_x Logical. Grand-mean centre X. Default FALSE. fe_interactions List NULL. Fixed-effect interaction terms. re_interactions List NULL. Random-effect interaction terms. re_cols Character vector. Random-slope columns. re_temporal Logical. Random slopes lag predictors. Default FALSE. K Integer. AR order. na_action Character. "listwise" currently supported. skip_lag Logical. Zero-fill lags across irregular time gaps. priors bvarnet_priors object. Defaults set_priors().

title: Build a Stan data list from a long-format data frame — to_stan_data
text: named list ready pass CmdStanModel$sample().
